{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_46c6e3fa55cf466e8fc05c7c","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Security Implementation\n2\t\n3\tThis document describes the security model for MTA My Way, including rate limiting, input validation, security headers, and data privacy measures.\n4\t\n5\t## Defense in Depth\n6\t\n7\tSecurity is implemented in layers:\n8\t\n9\t1. **Cloudflare WAF** (first line of defense)\n10\t2. **Hono middleware** (second line of defense)\n11\t3. **Zod input validation** (third line of defense)\n12\t\n13\t## Cloudflare WAF Configuration\n14\t\n15\tConfigure in the Cloudflare tunnel dashboard:\n16\t\n17\t- **Rate-limit rule**: 100 requests/minute per IP for `/api/*` paths\n18\t- Returns 429 with a `Retry-After` header\n19\t- Cloudflare Bot Management (free tier) filters automated abuse\n20\t\n21\t## Hono Rate Limiting\n22\t\n23\t**File**: `packages/server/src/middleware/rate-limiter.ts`\n24\t\n25\tIn-memory token bucket rate limiter:\n26\t- **Limit**: 60 requests/minute per IP\n27\t- **Algorithm**: Token bucket (1 token/second refill)\n28\t- **Client identification**: Uses `CF-Connecting-IP` header from Cloudflare tunnel, falls back to `X-Forwarded-For`\n29\t- **Memory management**: Periodic pruning of stale buckets every 5 minutes\n30\t\n31\t```typescript\n32\t// Example 429 response\n33\t{\n34\t  \"error\": \"Too many requests\",\n35\t  \"retryAfter\": 42\n36\t}\n37\t```\n38\t\n39\t**Note**: In-memory rate limiter resets on pod restart — acceptable for single-container deployment. Cloudflare WAF is the first line of defense.\n40\t\n41\t## Zod Input Validation\n42\t\n43\t**Files**:\n44\t- `packages/shared/src/schemas/push.ts` — Push notification schemas\n45\t- `packages/shared/src/schemas/commute.ts` — Commute analysis schemas\n46\t- `packages/server/src/middleware/validation.ts` — Validation helper\n47\t\n48\tAll API inputs are validated against Zod schemas before processing. Invalid payloads return 400 with structured error details:\n49\t\n50\t```typescript\n51\t// Example 400 response\n52\t{\n53\t  \"error\": \"Validation failed\",\n54\t  \"details\": [\n55\t    { \"field\": \"subscription.endpoint\", \"message\": \"Must be a valid URL\" },\n56\t    { \"field\": \"favorites[0].lines\", \"message\": \"Array must contain at least 1 item(s)\" }\n57\t  ]\n58\t}\n59\t```\n60\t\n61\t### Push Subscription Schemas\n62\t\n63\t- `pushSubscribeRequestSchema` — POST /api/push/subscribe\n64\t- `pushUnsubscribeRequestSchema` — DELETE /api/push/unsubscribe\n65\t- `pushUpdateRequestSchema` — PATCH /api/push/subscription\n66\t\n67\t### Commute Analysis Schema\n68\t\n69\t- `commuteAnalyzeRequestSchema` — POST /api/commute/analyze\n70\t\n71\t## Security Headers\n72\t\n73\t**File**: `packages/server/src/middleware/security-headers.ts`\n74\t\n75\tAll responses include:\n76\t\n77\t| Header | Value | Purpose |\n78\t|--------|-------|---------|\n79\t| `Content-Security-Policy` | `default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data:; connect-src 'self'; font-src 'self'; manifest-src 'self'; worker-src 'self'` | Prevents XSS attacks |\n80\t| `X-Content-Type-Options` | `nosniff` | Prevents MIME type sniffing |\n81\t| `X-Frame-Options` | `DENY` | Prevents clickjacking |\n82\t| `Referrer-Policy` | `strict-origin-when-cross-origin` | Controls referrer information |\n83\t| `Strict-Transport-Security` | `max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains` | HSTS for 1 year |\n84\t\n85\t### CSP Notes\n86\t\n87\t- `'unsafe-inline'` for styles is required for Tailwind CSS utility classes\n88\t- No `'unsafe-inline'` or `'unsafe-eval'` for scripts\n89\t- `data:` allowed for images (inline icons)\n90\t\n91\t## Trip Share Link TTL\n92\t\n93\t**File**: `packages/server/src/trip-lookup.ts`\n94\t\n95\tTrip share links (`/trip/:tripId`) have a 24-hour TTL enforced server-side:\n96\t\n97\t- First access records the timestamp\n98\t- Subsequent accesses check if 24 hours have elapsed\n99\t- Expired trips return 404\n100\t- Memory cleanup prunes stale entries every 5 minutes\n101\t\n102\t```typescript\n103\tconst TRIP_SHARE_TTL_MS = 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000; // 24 hours\n104\t```\n105\t\n106\t## Data Privacy (No PII Server-Side)\n107\t\n108\t**File**: `packages/server/src/push/subscriptions.ts`\n109\t\n110\tPush subscriptions are stored with privacy-by-design:\n111\t\n112\t- **Keyed by SHA-256 hash** of the subscription endpoint URL\n113\t- **No user identity** stored (no email, device ID, or account)\n114\t- **No location data** — geolocation is client-side only, never sent to backend\n115\t- **No PII in favorites** — favorites/commutes stored in browser localStorage, never sent to server\n116\t\n117\t```typescript\n118\tfunction hashEndpoint(endpoint: string): string {\n119\t  return createHash(\"sha256\").update(endpoint).digest(\"hex\");\n120\t}\n121\t```\n122\t\n123\t### Data Stored Server-Side\n124\t\n125\t| Data | Storage | PII? |\n126\t|------|---------|------|\n127\t| Push subscription keys | SQLite | No (hashed endpoint) |\n128\t| Favorite tuples (station/line/direction) | SQLite | No (transit data only) |\n129\t| Quiet hours settings | SQLite | No (preference only) |\n130\t| Morning scores | SQLite | No (preference only) |\n131\t\n132\t### Data Stored Client-Side Only\n133\t\n134\t- Full favorites list with labels\n135\t- Commute configurations\n136\t- Trip journal/history\n137\t- Fare tracking (OMNY cap)\n138\t- Tap history for context sorting\n139\t- Settings (theme, refresh interval, etc.)\n140\t- Geolocation coordinates\n141\t\n142\t## Security Checklist\n143\t\n144\t- [x] Cloudflare WAF: rate-limit rule for /api/* at 100 req/min per IP\n145\t- [x] Hono middleware: in-memory token bucket rate limiter (60 req/min per IP)\n146\t- [x] Zod Security Implementation\n\nThis document describes the security model for MTA My Way, including rate limiting, input validation, security headers, and data privacy measures.\n\n## Defense in Depth\n\nSecurity is implemented in layers:\n\n1. **Cloudflare WAF** (first line of defense)\n2. **Hono middleware** (second line of defense)\n3. **Zod input validation** (third line of defense)\n\n## Cloudflare WAF Configuration\n\nConfigure in the Cloudflare tunnel dashboard:\n\n- **Rate-limit rule**: 100 requests/minute per IP for `/api/*` paths\n- Returns 429 with a `Retry-After` header\n- Cloudflare Bot Management (free tier) filters automated abuse\n\n## Hono Rate Limiting\n\n**File**: `packages/server/src/middleware/rate-limiter.ts`\n\nIn-memory token bucket rate limiter:\n- **Limit**: 60 requests/minute per IP\n- **Algorithm**: Token bucket (1 token/second refill)\n- **Client identification**: Uses `CF-Connecting-IP` header from Cloudflare tunnel, falls back to `X-Forwarded-For`\n- **Memory management**: Periodic pruning of stale buckets every 5 minutes\n\n```typescript\n// Example 429 response\n{\n  \"error\": \"Too many requests\",\n  \"retryAfter\": 42\n}\n```\n\n**Note**: In-memory rate limiter resets on pod restart — acceptable for single-container deployment. Cloudflare WAF is the first line of defense.\n\n## Zod Input Validation\n\n**Files**:\n- `packages/shared/src/schemas/push.ts` — Push notification schemas\n- `packages/shared/src/schemas/commute.ts` — Commute analysis schemas\n- `packages/server/src/middleware/validation.ts` — Validation helper\n\nAll API inputs are validated against Zod schemas before processing. Invalid payloads return 400 with structured error details:\n\n```typescript\n// Example 400 response\n{\n  \"error\": \"Validation failed\",\n  \"details\": [\n    { \"field\": \"subscription.endpoint\", \"message\": \"Must be a valid URL\" },\n    { \"field\": \"favorites[0].lines\", \"message\": \"Array must contain at least 1 item(s)\" }\n  ]\n}\n```\n\n### Push Subscription Schemas\n\n- `pushSubscribeRequestSchema` — POST /api/push/subscribe\n- `pushUnsubscribeRequestSchema` — DELETE /api/push/unsubscribe\n- `pushUpdateRequestSchema` — PATCH /api/push/subscription\n\n### Commute Analysis Schema\n\n- `commuteAnalyzeRequestSchema` — POST /api/commute/analyze\n\n## Security Headers\n\n**File**: `packages/server/src/middleware/security-headers.ts`\n\nAll responses include:\n\n| Header | Value | Purpose |\n|--------|-------|---------|\n| `Content-Security-Policy` | `default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data:; connect-src 'self'; font-src 'self'; manifest-src 'self'; worker-src 'self'` | Prevents XSS attacks |\n| `X-Content-Type-Options` | `nosniff` | Prevents MIME type sniffing |\n| `X-Frame-Options` | `DENY` | Prevents clickjacking |\n| `Referrer-Policy` | `strict-origin-when-cross-origin` | Controls referrer information |\n| `Strict-Transport-Security` | `max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains` | HSTS for 1 year |\n\n### CSP Notes\n\n- `'unsafe-inline'` for styles is required for Tailwind CSS utility classes\n- No `'unsafe-inline'` or `'unsafe-eval'` for scripts\n- `data:` allowed for images (inline icons)\n\n## Trip Share Link TTL\n\n**File**: `packages/server/src/trip-lookup.ts`\n\nTrip share links (`/trip/:tripId`) have a 24-hour TTL enforced server-side:\n\n- First access records the timestamp\n- Subsequent accesses check if 24 hours have elapsed\n- Expired trips return 404\n- Memory cleanup prunes stale entries every 5 minutes\n\n```typescript\nconst TRIP_SHARE_TTL_MS = 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000; // 24 hours\n```\n\n## Data Privacy (No PII Server-Side)\n\n**File**: `packages/server/src/push/subscriptions.ts`\n\nPush subscriptions are stored with privacy-by-design:\n\n- **Keyed by SHA-256 hash** of the subscription endpoint URL\n- **No user identity** stored (no email, device ID, or account)\n- **No location data** — geolocation is client-side only, never sent to backend\n- **No PII in favorites** — favorites/commutes stored in browser localStorage, never sent to server\n\n```typescript\nfunction hashEndpoint(endpoint: string): string {\n  return createHash(\"sha256\").update(endpoint).digest(\"hex\");\n}\n```\n\n### Data Stored Server-Side\n\n| Data | Storage | PII? |\n|------|---------|------|\n| Push subscription keys | SQLite | No (hashed endpoint) |\n| Favorite tuples (station/line/direction) | SQLite | No (transit data only) |\n| Quiet hours settings | SQLite | No (preference only) |\n| Morning scores | SQLite | No (preference only) |\n\n### Data Stored Client-Side Only\n\n- Full favorites list with labels\n- Commute configurations\n- Trip journal/history\n- Fare tracking (OMNY cap)\n- Tap history for context sorting\n- Settings (theme, refresh interval, etc.)\n- Geolocation coordinates\n\n## Security Checklist\n\n- [x] Cloudflare WAF: rate-limit rule for /api/* at 100 req/min per IP\n- [x] Hono middleware: in-memory token bucket rate limiter (60 req/min per IP)\n- [x] Zod schemas for all API inputs: push subscription payload, commute analysis request\n- [x] Return 400 with structured error for invalid payloads\n- [x] CSP headers: default-src 'self', script-src 'self', style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'\n- [x] Security headers: X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy\n- [x] Trip share link TTL: 24h expiry enforced server-side\n- [x] Audit: verify no PII stored server-side (push subs keyed by hash, no user identity)\n","numLines":152,"startLine":1,"totalLines":152}}}

The file was removed from git tracking and added to `.gitignore`. The committed key was treated as compromised and rotated.\n\n**Actions taken**:\n1. Removed `packages/server/data/vapid-keys.json` and `packages/server/.test-db.sqlite` from git tracking\n2. Added both paths to `.gitignore` (`.test-db.sqlite` covered by `*.sqlite` pattern)\n3. Generated a fresh VAPID key pair for development use\n4. Production deployments use environment variables (`VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY`, `VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY`) instead of file-based configuration\n\n**Impact on clients**:\n- Clients that subscribed to push notifications using the old VAPID public key must re-subscribe\n- The subscription will be updated automatically on the next app launch/open\n- Push notifications will not be delivered to stale subscriptions using the old key\n\n**Current behavior** (see `packages/server/src/push/vapid.ts`):\n- Environment variables (`VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY`, `VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY`) take precedence\n- File fallback (`packages/server/data/vapid-keys.json`) is only used for local development when env vars are absent\n- Production deployments MUST use sealed secrets (see `bf-4012`)\n\n## Security Checklist","originalFile":"# Security Implementation\n\nThis document describes the security model for MTA My Way, including rate limiting, input validation, security headers, and data privacy measures.\n\n## Defense in Depth\n\nSecurity is implemented in layers:\n\n1. **Cloudflare WAF** (first line of defense)\n2. **Hono middleware** (second line of defense)\n3. **Zod input validation** (third line of defense)\n\n## Cloudflare WAF Configuration\n\nConfigure in the Cloudflare tunnel dashboard:\n\n- **Rate-limit rule**: 100 requests/minute per IP for `/api/*` paths\n- Returns 429 with a `Retry-After` header\n- Cloudflare Bot Management (free tier) filters automated abuse\n\n## Hono Rate Limiting\n\n**File**: `packages/server/src/middleware/rate-limiter.ts`\n\nIn-memory token bucket rate limiter:\n- **Limit**: 60 requests/minute per IP\n- **Algorithm**: Token bucket (1 token/second refill)\n- **Client identification**: Uses `CF-Connecting-IP` header from Cloudflare tunnel, falls back to `X-Forwarded-For`\n- **Memory management**: Periodic pruning of stale buckets every 5 minutes\n\n```typescript\n// Example 429 response\n{\n  \"error\": \"Too many requests\",\n  \"retryAfter\": 42\n}\n```\n\n**Note**: In-memory rate limiter resets on pod restart — acceptable for single-container deployment. Cloudflare WAF is the first line of defense.\n\n## Zod Input Validation\n\n**Files**:\n- `packages/shared/src/schemas/push.ts` — Push notification schemas\n- `packages/shared/src/schemas/commute.ts` — Commute analysis schemas\n- `packages/server/src/middleware/validation.ts` — Validation helper\n\nAll API inputs are validated against Zod schemas before processing. Invalid payloads return 400 with structured error details:\n\n```typescript\n// Example 400 response\n{\n  \"error\": \"Validation failed\",\n  \"details\": [\n    { \"field\": \"subscription.endpoint\", \"message\": \"Must be a valid URL\" },\n    { \"field\": \"favorites[0].lines\", \"message\": \"Array must contain at least 1 item(s)\" }\n  ]\n}\n```\n\n### Push Subscription Schemas\n\n- `pushSubscribeRequestSchema` — POST /api/push/subscribe\n- `pushUnsubscribeRequestSchema` — DELETE /api/push/unsubscribe\n- `pushUpdateRequestSchema` — PATCH /api/push/subscription\n\n### Commute Analysis Schema\n\n- `commuteAnalyzeRequestSchema` — POST /api/commute/analyze\n\n## Security Headers\n\n**File**: `packages/server/src/middleware/security-headers.ts`\n\nAll responses include:\n\n| Header | Value | Purpose |\n|--------|-------|---------|\n| `Content-Security-Policy` | `default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data:; connect-src 'self'; font-src 'self'; manifest-src 'self'; worker-src 'self'` | Prevents XSS attacks |\n| `X-Content-Type-Options` | `nosniff` | Prevents MIME type sniffing |\n| `X-Frame-Options` | `DENY` | Prevents clickjacking |\n| `Referrer-Policy` | `strict-origin-when-cross-origin` | Controls referrer information |\n| `Strict-Transport-Security` | `max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains` | HSTS for 1 year |\n\n### CSP Notes\n\n- `'unsafe-inline'` for styles is required for Tailwind CSS utility classes\n- No `'unsafe-inline'` or `'unsafe-eval'` for scripts\n- `data:` allowed for images (inline icons)\n\n## Trip Share Link TTL\n\n**File**: `packages/server/src/trip-lookup.ts`\n\nTrip share links (`/trip/:tripId`) have a 24-hour TTL enforced server-side:\n\n- First access records the timestamp\n- Subsequent accesses check if 24 hours have elapsed\n- Expired trips return 404\n- Memory cleanup prunes stale entries every 5 minutes\n\n```typescript\nconst TRIP_SHARE_TTL_MS = 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000; // 24 hours\n```\n\n## Data Privacy (No PII Server-Side)\n\n**File**: `packages/server/src/push/subscriptions.ts`\n\nPush subscriptions are stored with privacy-by-design:\n\n- **Keyed by SHA-256 hash** of the subscription endpoint URL\n- **No user identity** stored (no email, device ID, or account)\n- **No location data** — geolocation is client-side only, never sent to backend\n- **No PII in favorites** — favorites/commutes stored in browser localStorage, never sent to server\n\n```typescript\nfunction hashEndpoint(endpoint: string): string {\n  return createHash(\"sha256\").update(endpoint).digest(\"hex\");\n}\n```\n\n### Data Stored Server-Side\n\n| Data | Storage | PII? |\n|------|---------|------|\n| Push subscription keys | SQLite | No (hashed endpoint) |\n| Favorite tuples (station/line/direction) | SQLite | No (transit data only) |\n| Quiet hours settings | SQLite | No (preference only) |\n| Morning scores | SQLite | No (preference only) |\n\n### Data Stored Client-Side Only\n\n- Full favorites list with labels\n- Commute configurations\n- Trip journal/history\n- Fare tracking (OMNY cap)\n- Tap history for context sorting\n- Settings (theme, refresh interval, etc.)\n- Geolocation coordinates\n\n## Security Checklist\n\n- [x] Cloudflare WAF: rate-limit rule for /api/* at 100 req/min per IP\n- [x] Hono middleware: in-memory token bucket rate limiter (60 req/min per IP)\n- [x] Zod schemas for all API inputs: push subscription payload, commute analysis request\n- [x] Return 400 with structured error for invalid payloads\n- [x] CSP headers: default-src 'self', script-src 'self', style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'\n- [x] Security headers: X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy\n- [x] Trip share link TTL: 24h expiry enforced server-side\n- [x] Audit: verify no PII stored server-side (push subs keyed by hash, no user identity)\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":139,"oldLines":6,"newStart":139,"newLines":26,"lines":[" - Settings (theme, refresh interval, etc.)"," - Geolocation coordinates"," ","+## VAPID Key
